channel.title: Daily Briefing
channel.description: A daily briefing on weather, tech, health, business, theology, philosophy, and clear thinking.

item.title: Daily Briefing — 2026-03-20
item.description:
Weather

The atmospheric river that parked over the Fraser Valley this week is finally easing, but not before dumping 100–120 mm of rain and triggering a local state of emergency in Chilliwack. The Chilliwack River is approaching its expected crest, roughly 40 homes are under evacuation alert, and dikes are holding. Environment Canada has today at 10°C with a 40% chance of showers. Saturday similar at 9°C. Sunday climbs to 11°C before rain returns early next week. Trails saturated; BC River Forecast Centre has issued a Flood Watch.

Tech

Claude Code

Claude Code v2.1.81 shipped with a --bare flag for scripted calls that skips hooks, LSP, and plugin sync. v2.1.80 introduced inline plugin declarations in settings.json, CLI tool-usage detection for plugin tips, and effort frontmatter support for skills. The /loop command now handles recurring prompts at set intervals. Agent Teams remain in research preview. Voice mode added 10 new languages this month.

Cowork

Microsoft Copilot Cowork launched March 10, powered by Claude, for multi-step tasks across M365. Anthropic's Cowork now supports scheduled tasks and a new Customize section. Dispatch — remote control from phone — is live.

Claude Platform

Anthropic published a survey of ~81,000 people across 159 countries on AI attitudes. Sub-Saharan Africa and Asia are more optimistic; independent workers reported 3× the economic empowerment from AI versus salaried employees. The DOJ urged a federal court to reject Anthropic's preliminary injunction against the Pentagon's supply-chain risk designation.

Competitors

OpenAI has surpassed $25B in annualized revenue and is exploring a public listing. GPT-5.4 launched with a 1.05M token context window. The gap between frontier models continues to narrow. MCP is becoming the industry standard — OpenAI, Microsoft, and Google have adopted it.

Apple

Reviews consolidating from Apple's March product week. MacBook Air M5 is the standout value. MacBook Neo ($599) drawing attention as a Chromebook killer. iPhone 17e reviews mixed on C1X modem. Rumour: iPhone Fold in development; iOS 27 to focus on stability.

General Interest

Hacker News: "Show HN: Will my flight have Starlink?" (350 points). CVE-2026-3888 Snap privilege escalation to root. North Korean fake IT workers netting $500M/year. UK backtracking on AI copyright after artist outcry.

Health

AACR 2026 underway in San Diego. J&J's Icotyde cleared as first oral IL-23 blocker, projected $5B+ in sales. BioCryst got FDA approval for oral ORLADEYO for children. Biotech layoffs accelerating: Gossamer Bio -48%, Theravance shutting R&D, Evotec -800. Biopharma VC funding at $2.7B vs $3.8B same point last year.

Business

Markets

Fourth consecutive weekly decline. S&P 500 at 5,606, down 1.51%. Nasdaq fell 2.01%. Russell 2000 in correction territory. Brent topped $112/bbl after Iraq force majeure and Kuwaiti refinery drone strikes. Planet Labs surged 26% on breakeven Q4.

Canada Macro

Bank of Canada held at 2.25%. CPI eased to 1.8% but oil-driven gasoline spikes expected. Unemployment at 6.7%. Markets pricing in a possible hike by year-end.

US Macro

Fed held at 3.50–3.75% (11-1). Dot plot signals one cut this year. PCE inflation raised to 2.7%. Powell's term expires May; Warsh nominated. Global central banks in synchronized hold facing stagflationary pressure.

Psalm of the Day — Psalm 1

The Two Ways

1Blessed is the man who walks not in the counsel of the wicked, nor stands in the way of sinners, nor sits in the seat of scoffers; 2but his delight is in the law of the LORD, and on his law he meditates day and night. 3He is like a tree planted by streams of water that yields its fruit in its season, and its leaf does not wither. In all that he does, he prospers. 4The wicked are not so, but are like chaff that the wind drives away. 5Therefore the wicked will not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous; 6for the LORD knows the way of the righteous, but the way of the wicked will perish.

Scripture quotations are from the ESV® Bible (The Holy Bible, English Standard Version®), © 2001 by Crossway, a publishing ministry of Good News Publishers. Used by permission. All rights reserved.

Exegesis

Psalm 1 is a Torah psalm — a wisdom composition that functions as the editorial gateway to the entire Psalter. The psalm's architecture is binary: two ways, two outcomes, two images. The righteous are a tree, planted and fruitful; the wicked are chaff, weightless and wind-scattered. The opening ʾašrê ("blessed") is a declaration, not a prayer. The three verbs — walks, stands, sits — trace a progression from casual association to settled identification with wickedness. The Hebrew tôrâ carries the broader sense of "instruction." The tree image draws on ANE royal iconography but democratizes it. The closing yāda' ("knows") operates in its covenantal sense: intimate, committed relationship. The psalm offers no third option.

SEP — The Problem of Evil

plato.stanford.edu/entries/evil/

The problem of evil is the oldest challenge to theism. The entry distinguishes the logical problem from the evidential problem. Plantinga's Free Will Defense resolved the logical version. The evidential problem, sharpened by Rowe's argument from gratuitous suffering, proves harder. Theodicies fall into several families: soul-making, free will, and natural law. Skeptical theism argues we should not expect to understand God's reasons. Critics reply this undercuts all moral reasoning. The debate has shifted from compatibility in principle to whether specific patterns of suffering are better explained by theism or naturalism.

Cognitive Bias — Confirmation Bias

Confirmation bias is the tendency to search for, interpret, and recall information that confirms existing beliefs. Wason's 2-4-6 task demonstrated this: subjects tested hypotheses by seeking confirmation rather than falsification. The corrective: actively seek the strongest opposing view before concluding you're right.
